===Pokémon=== [[File:Leon Dynamax Band.png|250px|thumb|Leon's [[Dynamax Band]]]] [[File:Leon Inteleon Counter Shield.png|250px|left|thumb|Leon's Inteleon using Counter Shield]] As expected from a high-level Trainer, all of Leon's Pokémon are very powerful. Leon's [[party]] consists of several Pokémon originating from his [[Galar|homeland]], as well as his [[List of characters' first Pokémon|first Pokémon]] Charizard, some of which have been shown to be capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. This has allowed Leon to achieve impressive victories in the [[World Coronation Series]] and the [[Masters Eight Tournament]] against other powerful Trainers like [[Raihan]], [[Flint]], [[Alain]], and [[Diantha]]. Leon's adaptability is very similar to Ash's, being able to rapidly teach his Pokémon some improvised techniques, such as [[Counter Shield]] for {{p|Inteleon}}. Another point in common with Ash is that Leon is very close to his Pokémon. This allowed Leon to develop his relationship with {{TP|Leon|Eternatus}}, which left aside its more aggressive side and became a more docile Pokémon over time. In ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'', it was shown that Leon's training is based on his personal relationship with other Pokémon and training flying skills and strength skills with his Pokémon such as Charizard or {{p|Dragapult}}. The strength of Leon's Pokémon is put to the test during the [[Full Battle]] against Ash, where Leon faced Ash and his Pokémon with all the battle mechanics Ash had at his disposal, and was still able to achieve several important victories even with a type disadvantage. This listing is of Leon's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: ====On hand==== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |main=Leon's Charizard |stage1=Charmander |stage2=Charmeleon |pkmn=Charizard |giga=yes |type1=Fire |type2=Flying |img=Leon Charizard.png |cap=Leon's Charizard |img2=Leon Gigantamax Charizard.png |cap2=Leon's Gigantamax Charizard |epnum=JN012 |epname=Flash of the Titans! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Kenta Miyake |desc={{p|Charizard}} is Leon's [[List of characters' first Pokémon|first Pokémon]], as well as his ace and capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. It was first seen being used in Leon's battle against {{an|Lance}} in the finals of the [[World Coronation Series]] and won. In ''[[JN131|Paring Pokémon While Parrying!]]'', Charizard was Leon's sixth Pokémon during his Masters Eight Tournament match against Ash. After Gigantamaxing, Charizard clashed with Ash's Pikachu's {{m|10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt}} [[Z-Move]], which caused {{TP|Leon|Eternatus}} to awaken and appear at [[Wyndon Stadium]]. After being briefly recalled in favor of {{p|Cinderace}} in [[JN132|the next episode]], Charizard resumed its battle with Pikachu. Both Pokémon gave it their all, with Charizard ultimately falling and suffering its first loss in an official battle, thus causing Leon to lose the match.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Dragapult |type1=Dragon |type2=Ghost |img=Leon Dragapult.png |epnum=JN043 |epname=Sword and Shield: The Darkest Day! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=List of English voice actors{{!}}Daman Mills |vaennote=Dreepy |vaen2=Marc Thompson |vaen2note=Dragapult |desc={{p|Dragapult}} is Leon's second known Pokémon. In its debut appearance, Leon used it to battle a {{p|Coalossal}} that had [[Gigantamax]]ed alongside [[Ash's Pikachu]] and {{AP|Gengar}}, eventually taking it down. In ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'', Dragapult was used in Leon's training. Sometime later, Leon used Dragapult to confront a pair of {{p|Corviknight}} that attacked him and {{Ash}}. In ''[[JN122|It's... Champion Time!]]'', Dragapult was one of the three Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against [[Diantha]]. It was shown battling her {{p|Hawlucha}} and {{p|Tyrantrum}} before being defeated by the latter. In ''[[JN130|Toying With Your Motions!]]'', Leon used Dragapult as his fourth Pokémon in his Masters Eight Tournament battle against Ash, where it fought against {{AP|Dracovish}}, {{AP|Lucario}}, and {{AP|Dragonite}}. It was able to defeat [[Mega Evolution|Mega]] Lucario, before losing to Dragonite. Dragapult's known moves are {{m|Dragon Darts}}, {{m|Dragon Rush}}, {{m|Flamethrower}}, {{m|Thunderbolt}}, and {{m|Dragon Tail}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Rillaboom |giga=yes |type1=Grass |img=Leon Rillaboom.png |cap=Leon's Rillaboom |img2=Leon Gigantamax Rillaboom.png |cap2=Leon's Gigantamax Rillaboom |epnum=JN115 |epname=Curtain Up! Fight the Fights! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Rillaboom}} is Leon's third known Pokémon. Like Charizard, it is capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. It was the first Pokémon that Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] battle against [[Alain]]. It went up against his {{p|Chesnaught}} and won, only to lose to [[Alain's Charizard]] afterwards. In ''[[JN122|It's... Champion Time!]]'', Rillaboom was one of the three Pokémon Leon used in his Masters Eight Tournament match against [[Diantha]]. It was shown defeating her {{p|Tyrantrum}}, {{p|Aurorus}}, {{p|Gourgeist}}, and {{p|Goodra}}, before losing to {{TP|Diantha|Gardevoir}}. In ''[[JN130|Toying With Your Motions!]]'', Rillaboom was sent out to battle [[Ash's Dragonite]] as Leon's fifth Pokémon in the Masters Eight Tournament finals. In [[JN131|the next episode]], it battled and defeated Dragonite and {{AP|Sirfetch'd}}, before losing to {{AP|Dracovish}}. Rillaboom's known moves are {{m|Acrobatics}}, {{m|Drum Beating}}, {{m|High Horsepower}}, and {{m|Knock Off}}.
As a Gigantamax Rillaboom, it can use {{m|G-Max Drum Solo}}, {{m|Max Airstream}}, and {{m|Max Quake}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Cinderace |giga=yes |type1=Fire |img=Leon Cinderace.png |cap=Leon's Cinderace |img2=Leon Gigantamax Cinderace.png |cap2=Leon's Gigantamax Cinderace |epnum=JN129 |epname=A Flood of Torrential Gains! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen= |desc={{p|Cinderace}} is Leon's fourth known Pokémon. It was the first Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against {{AP|Pikachu}}. Using its {{a|Libero}} [[Ability]] to [[Type change|become]] a {{t|Ground}} type, Cinderace was able to make Ash [[recall]] Pikachu before being recalled itself. In ''[[JN131|Paring Pokémon While Parrying!]]'', Cinderace was sent out to battle {{AP|Dracovish}}, defeating it before being recalled for Charizard. In [[JN132|the next episode]], it was revealed to be capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing as it fought against Gigantamax Pikachu, only to be defeated. Cinderace's known moves are {{m|Scorching Sands}}, {{m|High Jump Kick}}, {{m|Pyro Ball}}, and {{m|Iron Head}}, and its [[Ability]] is {{a|Libero}}.
As a Gigantamax Cinderace, it can use {{m|G-Max Fireball}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Inteleon |gender=male |type1=Water |img=Leon Inteleon.png |epnum=JN129 |epname=A Flood of Torrential Gains! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Barrett Leddy |desc={{p|Inteleon}} is Leon's fifth known Pokémon. He was the second Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where he went up against {{AP|Gengar}}. Despite forming a [[Counter Shield]] against Gengar, Inteleon was soon defeated by Gengar after it [[Gigantamax]]ed. Inteleon's known moves are {{m|Snipe Shot}}, {{m|Dark Pulse}}, and {{m|Aqua Jet}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Mr. Rime |type1=Ice |type2=Psychic |img=Leon Mr Rime.png |epnum=JN129 |epname=A Flood of Torrential Gains! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Billy Bob Thompson |desc={{p|Mr. Rime}} is Leon's sixth known Pokémon. It was the third Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against {{AP|Gengar}}. Despite being struck by {{a|Cursed Body}}, Mr. Rime was able to defeat Gengar. In [[JN130|the next episode]], it fought {{AP|Sirfetch'd}} and [[Mega Evolution|Mega]] {{AP|Lucario}}, ultimately losing to the latter. Mr. Rime's known moves are {{m|Freeze-Dry}}, {{m|Psychic Terrain}}, {{m|Expanding Force}}, and {{m|Triple Axel}}.}} ====Escaped==== {{TrainerPoké |main=yes |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Eternatus |type1=Poison |type2=Dragon |img=Leon Eternatus.png |epnum=JN012 |epname=Flash of the Titans! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Eternatus}} was originally {{pkmn2|caught}} by [[Goh]] in ''[[JN045|Sword and Shield... The Legends Awaken!]]'' after Leon's failed attempt to do the same, and was subsequently sealed away by [[Professor Magnolia]]. In ''[[JN127|Just a Scone's Throw From Here!]]'', Leon was revealed to have worked on getting Eternatus used to human contact, which he had achieved to the point of it being comfortable with him. As such, Goh decided to entrust it with Leon, which he accepted. It escaped from its containment in [[Hammerlocke]] in ''[[JN131|Paring Pokémon While Parrying!]]'', and, after making a brief stop at [[Wyndon Stadium]] in [[JN132|the next episode]], returned to living in the wild to become Galar's protector.}} ====Temporary==== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Opal |pkmn=Alcremie |gender=female |type1=Fairy |img=Leon Alcremie.png |epnum=JN082 |epname=The Sweet Taste of Battle! |vajp= |vaen=Ryan Bartley |desc=Leon borrowed an {{p|Alcremie}} from [[Opal]] to take part in the [[All-Alcremie Decoration Challenge]] she had arranged. None of Alcremie's moves are known.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Corviknight |type1=Flying |type2=Steel |img=Leon Corviknight.png |cap=Corviknight |epnum=JN100 |epname=Meeting Up with the Monarch! |vajp= |vaen= |desc=During his training with {{Ash}}, Leon encountered a pair of {{p|Corviknight}} that were attacking other Pokémon. After calming one of them, Leon took it away so Ash could save the injured {{p|Rookidee}} in Corviknight's nest. None of Corviknight's moves are known.}}
